title: Inheritance PASS: [106] QA_PLAN_CONTROL_COMPILER_CERT.v1 (family_extension) → [105] QA_CYMATIC_CONTROL_CERT.v1 (domain_instance)
_fixture_purpose: Second certified inheritance edge in the QA spec graph. Proves that [105] QA Cymatics Control is a valid domain instantiation of [106] QA Plan-Control Compiler: (1) it correctly declares inherits_from='QA_PLAN_CONTROL_COMPILER_CERT.v1'; (2) its gate policy is compatible; (3) as a domain_instance, the failure algebra is domain-specific (physical cymatics fail types) — IC5 is inapplicable here; domain instances define their own fail algebra because they operate in a different ontological layer (physical state transitions, not compilation relations); (4) IC6 and IC7 are also inapplicable for domain_instance scope (no explicit logging fields or invariant refs in the cymatics control cert); (5) the scope transition 'family_extension → domain_instance' is valid. This cert proves the full three-level stack: [107] kernel → [106] compiler → [105] cymatics is a well-formed inheritance chain.

validation_checks.description: parent family schema version is a recognised QA cert family
child family schema version is a recognised QA cert family
child gate policy is superset of parent gate policy
failure algebra check — inapplicable for domain_instance scope
validation_checks.details: domain_instance defines its own physical fail algebra (cymatics transitions); not required to extend compiler fail types. extracted_parent.failure_algebra_types=[]; IC5 skipped.

validation_checks.description: logging contract check — inapplicable (no explicit logging fields in parent or child at this scope)
invariant ref check — inapplicable (no preserves_invariants in domain_instance)
